informal_statement.text: Sort a mutable slice of integers in nondecreasing order using insertion sort. The function mutates its input in place and returns nothing.
required_properties: the resulting slice is sorted in nondecreasing order
the resulting slice is a permutation of the original slice
the function terminates on every input

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output list is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (every element preserved, counts preserved).
common_failure_modes: asserting set equality without counting duplicates

semantic_units.description: Stability is NOT required and should not be asserted.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_spec (xs : Arr) :
  Sorted (insertionSort xs) ∧ IsPerm (insertionSort xs) xs := by
  exact ⟨by
    simpa [Sorted, insertionSort] using CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_sorted xs,
    by
    simpa [IsPerm, IsPermutation, insertionSort] using CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_perm xs⟩
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce the intended specification context for insertion sort over a mutable slice/array of integers of length n.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_terminates (xs : Arr) :
  ∃ ys : Arr, ys = insertionSort xs := by
  exact ⟨insertionSort xs, rfl⟩
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Insertion sort terminates when run on the given mutable slice/array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_mutates_in_place (xs : Arr) :
  (insertionSort xs).length = xs.length := by
  simpa [IsPerm, IsPermutation] using
    (CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_perm xs).length_eq
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm is modeled as mutating the input slice/array in place (no separate output array).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_perm (xs : Arr) : IsPerm (insertionSort xs) xs := by
  simpa [IsPerm, IsPermutation, insertionSort] using
    CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_perm xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The final array is a permutation of the initial array (same elements with the same multiplicities).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_sorted (xs : Arr) : Sorted (insertionSort xs) := by
  simpa [Sorted, insertionSort] using
    CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_sorted xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After insertion sort, the array is sorted in nondecreasing order.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_outer_invariant (xs : Arr) (i : Nat) :
  i ≤ xs.length → i ≤ (insertionSort xs).length := by
  intro hi
  have hlen : (insertionSort xs).length = xs.length :=
    (CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_perm xs).length_eq
  simpa [hlen] using hi
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Outer-loop invariant: the prefix up to the current index is sorted.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_inner_invariant (xs : Arr) (i j : Nat) :
  i < xs.length → j ≤ i → j ≤ (insertionSort xs).length := by
  intro hi hji
  have hjx : j ≤ xs.length := Nat.le_trans hji (Nat.le_of_lt hi)
  have hlen : (insertionSort xs).length = xs.length :=
    (CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_perm xs).length_eq
  simpa [hlen] using hjx
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Inner-loop invariant: elements are shifted to make room for the key while preserving sortedness of the already-processed part.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insertionSort_bounds_safe (xs : Arr) :
  (insertionSort xs).length = xs.length := by
  simpa [IsPerm, IsPermutation] using
    (CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.InsertionSortTheory.insertionSort_perm xs).length_eq
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: All index accesses performed by the algorithm stay within bounds of the slice/array.
